sanskrit: अयनेषु च सर्वेषु यथाभागमवस्थिताः । भीष्ममेवाभिरक्षन्तु भवन्तः सर्व एव हि ॥
hindiTranslation: आप सबकेसब लोग सभी मोर्चों पर अपनीअपनी जगह दृढ़ता से स्थित रहते हुए ही पितामह भीष्म की चारों ओर से रक्षा करें ॥
englishTranslation: Remain at your stations on every front and protect Bhishma from all sides.
essence: Victory begins with disciplined formation, not scattered strength.
speaker: Duryodhana
simpleMeaning: Duryodhana orders all his warriors to hold their positions and guard Bhishma from every side.
simpleInsight: The army's first move is pure coordination: hold the line, protect the key commander, do not break formation.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Duryodhana is rallying his side before the first clash. After praising his warriors and measuring the two armies, he gives a tactical command: every fighter must stay in position and shield Bhishma, the main pillar of their force.
modernRelevance: Before a tense group presentation, everyone starts drifting into their own panic. One person has to call the room back into formation: stay in your place, cover the weak spot, don't let the center collapse.
translationLiteral: अयनेषु (on the battlefronts) / च (and) / सर्वेषु (all) / यथाभागम् (according to each one's place) / अवस्थिताः (standing positioned) / भीष्मम् (Bhishma) / एव (only) / अभिरक्षन्तु (let them protect) / भवन्तः (you all) / सर्वे (all) / हि (indeed)
